---
title: "Export events to a table"
subtitle: "Convert an `EventSeries` to rows while retaining timing and origin metadata."
sidebar: cookbook
---

## Use when

You need word, OCR, ASR, or object events as rows for joining, inspection, or modeling.

```{python}
from natural_features.core.interchange import as_event_table
from natural_features.workflows import extract_features

result = extract_features(
    "The scene opens on a quiet room.",
    features=["text.tokenize"],
)
words = result.features["text.tokenize"]
table = as_event_table(words)

table.loc[:, ["onset_s", "offset_s", "duration_s", "label", "extractor_name"]]
```

## Keep the object too

The table is a copy for code that expects rows and columns. Keep the
`EventSeries` when you need its methods, its exact metadata, or the versioned
description of its fields stored in `schema`.

```{python}
{
    "object": type(words).__name__,
    "schema": words.schema,
    "metadata_keys": sorted(words.metadata),
}
```

## See also

[Tables and DataFrames](../concepts/table-interchange.qmd)
